channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: New data show TikTok engagement as a key indicator of long-term box office success
item.description: New data shows opening weekend matters less as TikTok buzz drives long-term box office. Studios and theaters are leaning into the trend as the industry rebounds from pandemic losses, strikes and streaming pressure.
item.content:encoded: New data shows opening weekend matters less as TikTok buzz drives long-term box office. Studios and theaters are leaning into the trend as the industry rebounds from pandemic losses, strikes and streaming pressure.

item.title: Tired of waiting for your EV to charge up? One Chinese company has a novel solution
item.description: Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.
item.content:encoded:
Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.

(Image credit: Tang Ke/VCG via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: The Labor Department wants to teach you to use AI more. Here's what we found
item.description: The short course provides solid basics for using AI. But it also misidentifies AI products, links out to bad advice and raises ethical concerns about the products it promotes
item.content:encoded:
The short course provides solid basics for using AI. But it also misidentifies AI products, links out to bad advice and raises ethical concerns about the products it promotes

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: A Polymarket trader made $300,000 betting on Biden's pardons, a new analysis shows
item.description: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.
item.content:encoded: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Millions of people are pretending to be AI chatbots — for fun
item.description: Websites like youraislopbores.me have become playgrounds for people looking for light relief in a bot-heavy world.
item.content:encoded:
Websites like youraislopbores.me have become playgrounds for people looking for light relief in a bot-heavy world.

(Image credit: Screenshot by NPR)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Man accused in Molotov cocktail attack of OpenAI CEO's home charged with attempted murder
item.description: No one was injured at Altman's home or the company offices, authorities said.
item.content:encoded:
No one was injured at Altman's home or the company offices, authorities said.

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How AI is getting better at finding security holes
item.description: Anthropic announced this week that its new model found security flaws in "every major operating system and web browser." Even before the news, AI models had gotten dramatically better at finding bugs.
item.content:encoded:
Anthropic announced this week that its new model found security flaws in "every major operating system and web browser." Even before the news, AI models had gotten dramatically better at finding bugs.

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: How governments have tried to hide information about the Iran war online
item.description: Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.
item.content:encoded:
Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.

(Image credit: Planet)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Why OpenAI bought 'SportsCenter for Silicon Valley'
item.description: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: ICE acknowledges it is using powerful spyware
item.description: In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress that the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.
item.content:encoded:
In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress that the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.

(Image credit: Octavio Jones)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block
